chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 464 of 2018 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Application in disposed of matter For hearing of CMA No.4565/2024 (151 CPC) 09.10.2024 Mr. Abdul Naeem Pirzada, Advocate for petitioners / applicants. Mr. Khuda Bux Chohan, Advocate for respondents / Sukkur Municipal Corporation. Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Assistant Advocate General Sindh..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Learned AAG Sindh has filed certain documents to show that the Appellate Tribunal Local Councils Sindh is still in existence and operational at Karachi; however, Mr. Zeeshan Akhter Khan, Chairman / Judge of the said Tribunal, who was holding the camp at the premises of District & Sessions Court, Sukkur, has since transferred or retired and the Tribunal is lying vacant since 5th March, 2024, and the directions of Hon’ble Chief Justice of this Court have already been issued through Registrar’s letter dated 21.05.2014 that the Chairman, Sindh Local Councils Tribunal, Karachi will act as link / incharge of the Anti- Encroachment Tribunal, Karachi during absence of its Presiding Officer on account of leave and vice versa, the Presiding Officer of the Anti- Encroachment Tribunal, Karachi is hearing such matters. Since this Court through its order dated 01.02.2024 has already directed the parties to approach the Appellate Tribunal Local Councils Sindh for redressal of their grievances, let the aforesaid Tribunal hear and decide the case of the petitioners after giving an opportunity of hearing to both the sides, within a period of 15 days from today to whom a copy of this order be sent immediately.

Zeeshan Akhter Khan, Chairman / Judge of the said Tribunal, who was holding the camp at the premises of District & Sessions Court, Sukkur, has since transferred or retired and the Tribunal is lying vacant since 5th March, 2024, and the directions of Hon’ble Chief Justice of this Court have already been issued through Registrar’s letter dated 21.05.2014 that the Chairman, Sindh Local Councils Tribunal, Karachi will act as link / incharge of the Anti- Encroachment Tribunal, Karachi during absence of its Presiding Officer on account of leave and vice versa, the Presiding Officer of the Anti- Encroachment Tribunal, Karachi is hearing such matters. Since this Court through its order dated 01.02.2024 has already directed the parties to approach the Appellate Tribunal Local Councils Sindh for redressal of their grievances, let the aforesaid Tribunal hear and decide the case of the petitioners after giving an opportunity of hearing to both the sides, within a period of 15 days from today to whom a copy of this order be sent immediately. Learned Counsel for the petitioners submits that he has already made an application before the said Tribunal. Let that application along with any other application to be filed by the petitioners, be heard and decided as above within above 15 days’ period with copy of the order sent to Additional Registrar of this Court. The listed application is disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
